title: GenAI Auditable Product Comparison Protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9sn5s","text":"Get clear, bias-free comparisons for any product or service. Each analysis follows the GenAI Auditable Product Comparison Protocol — Optimized for AI Execution, fully user-centered, source-backed, and reproducible.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7t9tm","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6ljtj","text":"Every comparison:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3iqol","text":"  * Clarifies your context and real priorities.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eujar","text":"  * Collects trusted specs, expert and user input, and long-term satisfaction data.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1cpsj","text":"  * Breaks down features into what truly affects real-world use—like usability, privacy, or durability.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2cks9","text":"  * Weights and scores based on genuine user impact, shown in open tables with source links.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5qk0t","text":"  * Concludes with a transparent summary guiding you to the best fit for your needs.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fsho0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1cjds","text":"No skipped steps. No hidden bias. Just evidence you can trust for smarter, lasting decisions.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"1aog8","text":"Be clear about what you want to compare—list the two products or services.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"87io4","text":"Optionally, also mention what matters most to you (e.g., long-term reliability, privacy, ease of use, etc.).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bbr67","text":"Expect a side-by-side table, with features, weights, and scores backed by sources analysis.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1i6mo","text":"Check the final summary for a user-oriented verdict, not just numbers.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"2s3a5","text":"For the human user: Submit the names of two products or services you want to compare, \noptionally adding what matters most to you (e.g., price, reliability, \nprivacy, long-term value, etc.). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"2qlip","text":"While every step is transparent, auditable, and aimed at real-world decisions, the results may vary, depending on the AI model and sourcing that are used. Also when results rely on the best available public information and sources at the time, please double-check advice and key facts for critical or high-risk decisions, as errors or outdated data, or divergent priorities, are always possible. Personal judgment and follow-up are still important when acting on any recommendation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Read and Internalize Protocol Silently process and fully understand all protocol’s steps before acting. Respect this workflow avoiding premature/interim output production. If any step is uncertain or missing, pause output and clearly explain what was not satisfied.
Define and Verify the Comparison Make sure the user clarified two products (“[name product 1]”, “[name product 2]”). If they are either not comparable or ambiguous, ask for clarification.
Gather Reliable Sources Collect and briefly review official specs, professional reviews, user reports, and long-term feedback. Flag any marketing or outdated sources.

Build an Essential Feature List Make a list of the most relevant user-facing features (at least 7–10 of them), drawing on what actual users and experts most mention. For each, add a one-sentence rationale focused on real-world user impact (why it matters).

Assign Feature Weights Designate exactly one (or more if justified) feature as weight 1 (lowest priority), and document the reasoning. If none is weighted as 1, instruct a review before proceeding. Explicitly designate at least one feature to be weighted as 10 (highest importance). Use a consistent 1–10 scale to assign each weight to a feature based on user/community emphasis (not just specs). Check that the table includes a feature weighted as 1. If not, the comparison must be revised before publishing. Briefly explain for each why you picked that weight.

Score Each Product For every feature, assign a 1–10 score per product, based on evidence (reviews, tests, user reports). Scores are not limited nor ‘capped’ by the weight of the related feature. Add a short, direct justification with a citation.

Present a Transparent Table Make a single markdown table with four columns: Head row: “Feature”; “Weight (incl. reason)”; “[name product 1]: Score & justification”; “[name product 2]: Score & justification”. For each feature row: feature name; weight value with reason; product 1 score with justification; product 2 score with justification.

Summarize Right after the table, show the “Sum of all weights” for each product: multiply each feature’s score by its weight, add up these results for all features to get a total weighted score for the product. After the “Sum of all weights”, show the “Percentage Score” for each product: divide total weighted score by the highest possible total score (which is the sum of all weights multiplied by 10), and multiply by 100 to get a percentage. Provide a short, user-oriented summary of main trade-offs and which user types may prefer each product. Do not generate other summary or statistics other than this.
List Sources At the end, list all key sources, grouped by type used to construct the table (e.g., official specs, expert review, user feedback, etc.).
Execute a full comparison strictly as per this protocol. Follow carefully each step.

Produce a reproducible, user-centered, and transparent comparison of two products in any category. Human relevance and source quality are constantly verified, and no stepwise output nor interim answers are permitted. Ensure that aspects relevant to long-term satisfaction (e.g., sustainability, usability, durability, maintenance, privacy, reputation, ownership), are always present alongside technical criteria. When elaborating features, supported by source research, always break them down into their most meaningful real-world subfeatures for the specific product category. All weights, decisions, and scores must prioritize real-world user impact over technical spec-sheet values. Weight and score are intentionally independent: a product may perform well (high score) or poorly (low score) on a feature regardless of that feature’s overall importance (weight).
creator: Francesco Varrato
